Title: Galactan mobilization during carbon starvation compromises plant cell wall-mediated resistance to fungal infection

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; pgm background for starvation comparison
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in rosette leaves after extended night; also induced during dark phase in pgm from published transcriptomics
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rosette leaves under dark-induced starvation/extended night
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cell wall galactose released from pectic galactan
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Loss-of-function bgal1 alleles showed partial reduction of starvation-induced wall galactose release, indicating functional redundancy with other BGALs such as BGAL4. The released galactose pool is GALS-dependent and inferred to derive from RG-I β-1,4-galactan rather than xyloglucan.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Galactose mobilization helps carbon economy during starvation but compromises cell wall-based penetration resistance to fungal infection.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Reducing BGAL1 activity, likely together with other redundant BGALs, could help preserve wall galactan and resistance under carbon starvation, but may limit recyclable sugar supply.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; loss-of-function T-DNA mutant analysis; cell wall monosaccharide profiling under extended night
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Extended night/dark-induced carbon starvation; periodic starvation in starch-deficient pgm
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starvation-induced release of galactose from leaf cell walls, contributing to galactan mobilization.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides recyclable carbon during starvation; wall galactan also supports penetration resistance against Colletotrichum higginsianum.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics referenced from prior studies (Usadel et al., 2008; Engelsdorf et al., 2017); no accession reported in this paper
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf rosette cell wall, especially pectic RG-I galactan side chains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BGAL1 transcript is strongly induced by extended night. 2. bgal1 loss-of-function mutants show less reduction of wall galactose during starvation than Col-0. 3. BGAL1 therefore contributes to mobilization of pectic galactan-derived galactose under starvation, a process linked to weakened penetration resistance.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; pgm background for starvation comparison
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in rosette leaves after extended night; reported induced/secreted upon carbohydrate starvation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rosette leaves under dark-induced starvation/extended night
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cell wall galactose released from pectic galactan
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: BGAL4 was previously reported to be secreted upon carbohydrate starvation. Loss-of-function mutants partially reduced starvation-induced galactose release, supporting redundancy. Evidence indicates released galactose derives from RG-I galactan side chains.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Mobilization of wall galactose benefits starvation survival but weakens preformed wall defense against fungal penetration.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Targeting BGAL4 together with BGAL1 may be useful to maintain wall galactan and pathogen resistance in low-carbon conditions.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; loss-of-function T-DNA mutant analysis; cell wall monosaccharide profiling under extended night
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Extended night/dark-induced carbon starvation; periodic starvation in pgm
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes starvation-induced release of galactose from leaf cell walls, contributing to galactan degradation.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Provides recyclable carbon during starvation; intact galactan contributes to pathogen penetration resistance.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics referenced from prior studies; no accession reported
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaf rosette cell wall RG-I galactan side chains
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. BGAL4 is >10-fold induced by extended night. 2. bgal4 mutants release less wall galactose during starvation than wild type. 3. BGAL4 contributes to starvation-dependent degradation of pectic galactan.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; pgm; uge1 uge3; pgm uge1 uge3
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Strongly induced in rosette leaves by extended night and elevated in pgm under nocturnal starvation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rosette leaves at end of night and during extended night starvation
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: UDP-galactose/galactose salvage-derived carbon pool supporting wall galactose reutilization
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Although induced by starvation and proposed to function in carbohydrate catabolism, uge1 uge3 double mutants did not accumulate UDP-galactose and showed no altered pathogen susceptibility, indicating redundancy with other UGE isoforms.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order disruption of UGE family may cause severe dwarf phenotypes per prior literature.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Not an ideal standalone engineering target for this trait because UGE1 function in starvation-induced galactose salvage is redundant.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course; double-mutant analysis; LC-MS/MS quantification of UDP-galactose and UDP-glucose; pathogen infection assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Extended night starvation; periodic starvation in pgm
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Starvation-induced expression suggests participation in galactose salvage, but loss of UGE1 with UGE3 is dispensable for maintaining UDP-galactose levels and susceptibility phenotype under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Recycled galactose can be converted into central carbohydrate metabolism intermediates.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics referenced from prior studies; LC-MS/MS nucleotide sugar profiling in this study
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGE1 transcript increases during extended night and in pgm. 2. uge1 uge3 mutants do not show UDP-galactose backlog under starvation. 3. UGE1 is therefore induced but functionally redundant in starvation-associated galactose salvage.

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; pgm; uge1 uge3; pgm uge1 uge3
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Induced in rosette leaves by extended night with lower absolute expression than UGE1; elevated in pgm under starvation
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Rosette leaves during end of night and extended night starvation
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Despite transcriptional induction, uge1 uge3 double mutants showed no significant effect on UDP-galactose pools or pathogen susceptibility under starvation, indicating redundancy with other UGE isoforms.
Common_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Higher-order UGE perturbation may incur severe growth defects according to cited prior studies.
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited standalone value because of strong redundancy; could be considered only in broader pathway manipulation with caution.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course; double-mutant analysis; LC-MS/MS quantification of nucleotide sugars; pathogen infection assay
Common_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Extended night starvation; periodic starvation in pgm
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Associated with starvation-induced galactose salvage transcriptionally, but not required with UGE1 for maintaining UDP-galactose homeostasis under tested conditions.
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports reutilization of released galactose into general carbohydrate metabolism.
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics referenced from prior studies; LC-MS/MS nucleotide sugar profiling in this study
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGE3 is induced by starvation, though less strongly than UGE1. 2. Combined loss with UGE1 does not alter UDP-galactose depletion pattern under starvation. 3. UGE3 is a redundant component of galactose salvage rather than a decisive determinant.

Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR time course; overexpression analysis; cell wall monosaccharide profiling; fungal penetration assay; fungal DNA qPCR
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Cell wall galactan acts as a carbon reserve during starvation and contributes to penetration resistance against Colletotrichum higginsianum.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Highest at end of day; strongly reduced at end of night and during extended night; nearly depleted in pgm at end of night
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: cell wall galactan / galactose content in pectic RG-I
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Strong candidate to increase wall galactan reserves and maintain fungal penetration resistance under carbon starvation; overexpression was sufficient to suppress starvation-induced susceptibility.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Promotes synthesis and maintenance of RG-I β-1,4-galactan; overexpression elevates wall galactose and rescues starvation-impaired penetration resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control point for leaf galactan abundance under starvation-responsive conditions, though not explicitly termed rate-limiting.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Starvation downregulates GALS1, attenuating new galactan synthesis while BGAL-mediated degradation proceeds. Overexpression does not block release itself but increases the remaining galactan pool after starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed at end of day in leaves; strongly reduced at end of night and extended night
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Transfers galactosyl residues from UDP-galactose to elongate β-1,4-galactan side chains of pectic RG-I in the Golgi.
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: No obvious visible phenotypes reported for altered galactan content in cited studies; however, preserving galactan may reduce the recyclable sugar pool during starvation.
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Overexpression line promoter not specified in provided text
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Extended night/dark-induced starvation; periodic nighttime starvation in pgm; fungal infection with Colletotrichum higginsianum
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. GALS1 expression is high at end of day but strongly repressed during starvation and in pgm. 2. GALS1 overexpression increases cell wall galactose and keeps it above wild-type levels even after extended night. 3. Elevated GALS1 suppresses starvation-induced fungal entry and pgm hypersusceptibility, linking galactan biosynthesis to penetration resistance.
Pathway_Genes.Metabolic_Step_Position: Downstream polymer assembly step in cell wall galactan biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Transcriptomics from prior studies referenced; qRT-PCR and metabolite/cell wall profiling in this study

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; pgm; uge1 uge3; pgm uge1 uge3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; double mutant analysis; LC-MS/MS nucleotide sugar analysis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: UDP-glucose from UDP-galactose (catabolic direction inferred under salvage conditions)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports reutilization of wall-derived galactose carbon in central metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during extended night and in pgm nocturnal starvation
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: reutilizable carbohydrate derived from wall galactose salvage
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited as single target because pathway function is redundant.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Contributes putatively to epimerization within galactose salvage but is not uniquely required under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Not established; paper argues against decisive unique control due to redundancy.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Included because it is directly experimentally interrogated in Results as part of salvage pathway; functional effect on nutrient metabolite was not decisive.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts salvage intermediate into central UDP-sugar pool supporting metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Family-wide disruption can cause strong developmental defects per cited literature.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGE1 is transcriptionally induced by starvation. 2. Loss with UGE3 does not cause UDP-galactose accumulation. 3. UGE1 is redundant in starvation-associated galactose salvage.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS nucleotide sugar profiling; referenced transcriptomics

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Col-0; pgm; uge1 uge3; pgm uge1 uge3
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: qRT-PCR; double mutant analysis; LC-MS/MS nucleotide sugar analysis
Pathway_Genes.Primary_Product: UDP-glucose from UDP-galactose (catabolic direction inferred under salvage conditions)
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports salvage of wall-derived galactose into usable carbohydrate metabolism.
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Induced during extended night and in pgm, lower abundance than UGE1
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite: reutilizable carbohydrate derived from wall galactose salvage
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Limited by redundancy with other UGE isoforms.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Putatively contributes to starvation-associated UDP-gal/UDP-Glc interconversion but is not uniquely required with UGE1.
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Experimentally studied but shown to be dispensable together with UGE1 for measurable salvage outputs under tested conditions.
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Converts salvage intermediate into central UDP-sugar pool supporting metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Potential developmental costs if broader UGE redundancy is removed.
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. UGE3 is induced by starvation. 2. Combined loss with UGE1 does not alter UDP-galactose or pathogen susceptibility. 3. UGE3 is a redundant salvage enzyme rather than a key determinant.
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: LC-MS/MS nucleotide sugar profiling; referenced transcriptomics
